info.description:
Runtype is an AI product development platform with powerful AI-driven automation capabilities.

## Authentication

All API endpoints require authentication using either:
- **API Key**: Use a Runtype API key in the Authorization header
- **Clerk Session**: Use a Clerk session token for dashboard authentication

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>
```
info.title: Runtype API

get.summary: Get the published version for an agent

400.description: Version does not belong to this agent

get.description: Get all agents for the authenticated user with filtering and cursor pagination.

post.description: Create a new agent with optional capabilities and configuration. Supports runtype, external, and claude_managed agent types.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful customer support agent

value.description: An AI agent for customer support
value.name: My New Agent

post.description: Proxy-fetch an external agent card from a URL, avoiding CORS issues. The URL is validated against SSRF attacks.

502.description: Failed to fetch agent card from remote

delete.description: Delete an agent and cascade-delete its capabilities.

get.description: Get an agent by ID including its capabilities and configuration.

put.description: Update an existing agent. Supports partial updates — only provided fields are changed. Config updates are deep-merged.

post.description: Resolve a pending tool approval on a paused agent execution (approved/denied). Streams a Server-Sent Events response when streamResponse is true (default); otherwise returns a JSON result.

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of agent execution events (or a JSON result when streamResponse is false)

post.summary: Resolve a pending agent tool approval

post.description: Add a capability (flow, sub-agent, or tool binding) to an agent.

409.description: Tool name already exists for this agent

delete.description: Remove a capability (flow, sub-agent, or tool binding) from an agent.

post.description: Execute an agent (Quick Agent mode) with a message array. Streams a Server-Sent Events response when streamResponse is true (default); otherwise returns a JSON result.

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of agent execution events (or a JSON result when streamResponse is false)

400.description: Invalid agent ID or request body

get.description: List execution history for an agent with cursor pagination. Supports cross-agent subagent drill-down via parentExecutionId or executionId filters.
Get full details for a single agent execution including final output, iteration details, and loop configuration.
List tool calls for a single agent execution. Returns a compact payload without input/output JSON — use the detail endpoint for full payloads.
Get full payload for a single tool call including input parameters and output result.

Export a fully-resolved, self-contained agent definition that the @runtypelabs/runtime package can consume directly at boot. Capabilities, flows, and nested sub-agents are inlined recursively (up to 3 levels). MCP credential values are replaced with secret-name references.

post.description: Snapshot the current live agent state and promote it to a published version.

Re-fetch the agent card from the configured agentCardUrl (or derived A2A well-known URL) and update the cached copy. Detects skill changes, auto-applies roles to new skills, and returns a change report.

400.description: Invalid agent ID, not an external agent, or no agent card URL

502.description: Failed to fetch agent card from remote

post.description: Resume a paused agent execution by supplying local tool outputs. Streams a Server-Sent Events response when streamResponse is true (default); otherwise returns a JSON result.

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of agent execution events (or a JSON result when streamResponse is false)

post.description: Generate speech audio for the given text using the agent voice configuration (ElevenLabs TTS).

value.text: Hello, how can I help you today?

400.description: Invalid agent ID or missing text parameter

403.description: Insufficient permissions or agent does not support voice

get.description: Get all record results for the current user with filtering and pagination. When a `days` parameter is provided, returns aggregated daily execution analytics instead of paginated results.

200.description: Record results or aggregated daily analytics

get.description: Get user analytics stats (records, prompts, flows, executions, evals, agents, products count). Supports optional `from` and `to` date filters.
Serve a preview-rendering HTML asset inline with locked-down CSP.
Serve an asset from R2 storage with proper headers. Public assets served immediately; private assets require signed URL.

200.description: Asset content. The Content-Type reflects the stored asset (e.g. image/png, image/jpeg), defaulting to application/octet-stream.

403.description: Access denied or must use storage domain

get.description: Get the current user and organization info from the Clerk session.

post.description: Cancels a running or queued batch execution.
Submits a batch of records for flow execution. Checks daily execution limits and feature gating.

flowByRecordIds.description: Submits a batch that executes a saved flow against a set of existing record IDs. Requires flowId (or flowDefinition/agentId) plus a non-empty recordIds (or records) array.
flowByRecordIds.summary: Run a saved flow over existing records

get.description: Returns the current status and progress of a batch execution.
Returns a summary of active and recently completed batch executions for the current user.
Returns the total cost for a specific record within a batch execution.
get.summary: Get per-record cost for a batch

get.description: Returns per-record step results for a batch execution, ordered as a timeline.
get.summary: Get step results for a batch record

get.description: Returns the total cost across all records, flow steps, agent executions, and nested subagents.
Establishes a WebSocket connection for real-time progress updates on a batch execution.
Get all client tokens for the authenticated user's tenant.

post.description: Create a new client token for browser-to-API communication. Returns the plain token value once.

createClientToken.description: Create a new client token for embedding chat widgets.

config.welcomeMessage: Hello! How can I help you today?

delete.description: Delete a client token by ID.

get.description: Get a client token by ID with conversation count.

put.description: Update fields on an existing client token.

config.welcomeMessage: Welcome! How can I assist you?

404.description: Client token or referenced flow not found

get.description: List conversations associated with a client token using offset pagination.

post.description: Generate a new token value for an existing client token. Invalidates the previous token.

get.description: Get the plain token value. Returns 400 with a hint if the token was created before token values were stored — use POST /{id}/regenerate to create a fresh value.

400.description: Invalid ID, or token value not stored (legacy token)

post.description: Send a message for a browser client-token chat session. The request streams flow/agent events over Server-Sent Events. `turnId` and `submitMode` are optional and let newer Persona clients suppress stale interrupted responses; older clients can omit both fields.

200.description: SSE stream of chat execution events

403.description: Client token inactive or origin not allowed

404.description: Referenced flow or agent not found

409.description: Client tools registry fingerprint was not found; resend full clientTools[]. The runtime response may include `clientToolsFingerprint` for retry correlation.

post.description: Record message-level (`upvote` / `downvote` / `copy`, requires `messageId`) or conversation-level (`csat` 1-5, `nps` 0-10, requires `rating`) feedback for a client-token session. Idempotent per (session, type, message): a matching prior submission is updated. Expired-but-active sessions are accepted so post-conversation surveys still land.

400.description: Validation error or invalid session ID format

403.description: Session deactivated, client token inactive, or origin mismatch

post.description: Exchange a client token for a browser chat session. Validates the token + request `Origin`, resolves the target flow or agent, and returns the session id plus widget display config. Pass an existing `sessionId` to reuse a prior conversation record.

default.summary: Initialize with the token default flow

id.description: Resolved flow or agent ID for this session.

sessionId.description: The created (or resumed) client session ID.

400.description: Validation error / flow required or not allowed

403.description: Client token inactive or origin not allowed

404.description: Referenced flow or agent not found

post.description: Session-authenticated sibling of `POST /v1/dispatch/resume` for browser client-token sessions. Resumes a paused local-tool (WebMCP / clientTools) execution by supplying `toolOutputs` keyed by the per-call `toolCallId` (or tool name, legacy). Streams the continued flow/agent events over Server-Sent Events. Does not count against execution limits.

default.summary: Resume with a single tool output

200.description: SSE stream of the resumed execution events

403.description: Client token inactive, origin mismatch, or execution belongs to another user

404.description: No paused execution found for the executionId

get.description: Get all context templates for the authenticated user with optional type/name filtering and cursor pagination.

post.description: Create a new context template. The config is validated based on the template type.

createContextTemplate.description: Creates an api-call context template. The handler parses the body with createContextTemplateSchema (type, name, config, optional organizationId) and then validates config against apiCallConfigSchema for type 'api-call', which requires config.http.url and config.outputVariable.

delete.description: Delete a context template by ID.

get.description: Get a specific context template by ID.

put.description: Update a context template. When config is provided it is validated based on the template type.

updateContextTemplate.description: Updates an api-call context template. updateContextTemplateSchema is createContextTemplateSchema.partial(); when config is provided the handler validates it against the configSchema for the (possibly inherited) type. Including type 'api-call' makes config validation deterministic; config must satisfy apiCallConfigSchema (config.http.url + config.outputVariable required).

createConversation.description: Creates a new conversation record (type 'conversation', source 'app').

updateConversation.description: Updates an existing conversation. All fields optional; metadata is merged; messages replaces the full message array.

messages.content: Sure! What kind of function would you like?

get.description: List all data connections for the authenticated user/org.

post.description: Create a connect session that opens the provider hosted connect UI.
Sync connections from StackOne into the local DB. Called by the dashboard after the Connect popup closes.

delete.description: Delete a data connection by ID.

get.description: Get a single data connection by ID.
List available tools for a specific data connection.

post.description: Main dispatch endpoint for flow and agent execution with record resolution. Supports streaming via SSE or synchronous JSON responses.

messages.content: You are a helpful customer support assistant for a SaaS company. Be professional, empathetic, and solution-oriented.
I've been charged twice this month for my subscription. Can you help me understand what happened?
I sincerely apologize for the billing error. Let me look into this right away. I can see you're on our Enterprise plan. Can you provide the dates of both charges so I can verify them in our system?
Yes, I was charged on the 1st and then again on the 15th. Both charges were for $299.

config.userPrompt: Say hello to {{_record.name}}! This is flow {{_flow.name}} (ID: {{_flow.id}}) executed at {{_execution.timestamp}} by user {{_user.id}}.

config.systemPrompt: You are an expert problem solver. Think through problems step by step.
config.userPrompt:
Solve this multi-step problem:

1. Calculate the compound interest on $10,000 at 5% annually for 3 years
2. If inflation is 2% per year, what is the real purchasing power?
3. Compare this to a simple interest calculation
4. Provide a recommendation

Show your reasoning process clearly.

config.systemPrompt: You are an expert problem solver.
config.userPrompt:
Analyze the same problem with detailed reasoning:

1. Calculate the compound interest on $10,000 at 5% annually for 3 years
2. If inflation is 2% per year, what is the real purchasing power?

virtualRecord.description: Dispatch - Create Virtual Record with Flow
virtualRecord.summary: Dispatch - Create Virtual Record with Flow

post.description: Submits an approval decision for a paused execution awaiting human-in-the-loop approval.

value.reason: Tool execution verified and safe to proceed

post.summary: Approve or deny a pending approval

post.description: Unified continuation endpoint that handles both tool-output resumption and approval decisions.
Resumes a paused flow or agent execution by providing tool outputs. Does not count against execution limits.

messages.content: Please continue processing with this data.

streamResponse.description: Whether to stream the response via SSE

toolOutputs.description: Local tool outputs for the paused execution. Key each output by the per-call `toolCallId` from the `flow_await` / `agent_await` event (preferred, and required to disambiguate parallel calls to the same tool), or by tool name (legacy; collapses same-tool parallel calls onto one slot).

post.description: Semantic search over the published Runtype documentation corpus. Returns a synthesized answer with citations. Use this for prose/how-to questions; for deterministic schemas and build guidance use the /v1/documentation endpoints.

get.description: Retrieve platform documentation, schemas, and reference material by topic, template ID, or vendor slug.

post.description: Retrieve build instructions and platform guidance for a given task.

buildProduct.description: Retrieves step-by-step build instructions for a product, based on a template. Backs the get_build_instructions MCP tool. The `task` field must be one of: build-product, generate-flow, explain-capabilities.

value.description: A customer support chatbot that answers questions from a knowledge base
value.name: Support Bot

post.description: Analyze step results for keyword/phrase occurrences across evals.

analyzeSteps.description: Search step outputs across an eval group for keyword/phrase occurrences (regex-capable), reporting per-term occurrence metrics.

get.description: List all eval batches for the current user with optional filtering and pagination.

post.description: Compare aggregate metrics across eval batches in a group.

compareEvals.description: Compare aggregate metrics across all eval batches sharing an evalGroupId, optionally filtered to specific step IDs.

post.description: Compare results for a specific record across eval batches in a group.

compareRecord.description: Compare results for one specific record across every eval batch in a group, optionally filtered to specific steps.

get.description: Get all eval batches in a group for comparison.

post.description: Execute virtual eval with record/messages input and stream results via multiplexed SSE. All eval configs execute simultaneously in parallel.
Submit a flow or agent for evaluation with model/parameter overrides. Supports both single eval and multi-model eval requests.

adHocRecords.description: Submit an evaluation using inline virtual records (name/type/metadata) that are not stored in the database, instead of recordIds.

multiModelEval.description: Submit several evaluation runs in one request (evalConfigs[]) to compare models on the same flow + records. All runs share an auto-generated evalGroupId.

singleEval.description: Submit a single evaluation run against a saved flow and records, with per-step model/parameter overrides.

toolVariations.description: Compare prompt behaviour across tool configurations (no tools, single tool, multiple parallel tools, required usage) via stepOverrides.tools in each evalConfig.

schema.description: Eval-group submission (when evalConfigs[] supplied) or a single batch submission.

get.description: Get detailed results for a specific eval batch including step results and tool calls.

post.description: Submit feedback (upvote/downvote/copy/csat/nps) on a message or conversation.

csatRating.description: Conversation-level CSAT feedback requires a rating between 1 and 5 and does not need a messageId.

upvoteMessage.description: Message-level feedback (upvote/downvote/copy) requires messageId. recordId/flowId, when supplied, must be valid TypeIDs and are ownership-checked by the handler.

get.description: Returns all steps stored in the flow config JSONB column, sorted by order.
get.summary: Get all flow steps for a flow

publishFlowVersion.description: Publishes a specific version of a flow, making it the active production version. The versionId must be a flow version TypeID (flowver_ prefix) belonging to the flow named in the path.

get.summary: Get the published version for a flow

400.description: Version does not belong to this flow

post.description: Stores an API key for the specified integration provider. Storing a NEW integration key requires the BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) entitlement; rotating a key that already exists for the provider is always allowed.

configureIntegrationProvider.description: Stores an API key for a specific integration provider (e.g. posthog). Storing a new provider key requires the BYOK entitlement; rotating an existing key is always allowed.

post.description: Reserve a new integration row server-side. Returns a pending row scoped to the authenticated owner whose id may be embedded in provider manifests before credentials are supplied.

200.description: List of integrations in the category

Get all Cloudflare Vectorize configurations for the authenticated user.

post.description: Create a new Cloudflare Vectorize configuration for the authenticated user.

production.description: Store a Cloudflare Vectorize configuration. The API token is encrypted before storage. timeout is optional (defaults to 30000ms).

post.description: Create a new Vectorize index in a Cloudflare account using provided or stored credentials.

newIndex.description: indexName must be kebab-case; dimensions 1-10000; metric one of cosine, euclidean, dot-product.

post.description: List all Vectorize indexes in a Cloudflare account using provided or stored credentials.

directCredentials.description: Provide accountId + apiToken directly, or supply configId to use stored credentials.

post.description: Test the connection to a Cloudflare Vectorize configuration before saving it.

testConnection.description: Validates connectivity to a Vectorize index using the same fields as create. timeout is optional.

assembleProduct.description: Assembles a complete product from a validated Full Product Object (FPO): the product, an agent capability, and a chat surface routed to that capability. The route request schema is a permissive placeholder (z.record); the handler validates the body against the canonical fullProductObjectSchema from @runtypelabs/shared, which requires version, product, capabilities (each with id/name/description and exactly one of flow|agent|existingFlowId|existingAgentId), tools, surfaces (each with id/name/type/config/routes), and _meta.

A2A Protocol v1.0 JSON-RPC 2.0 transport endpoint. The request body is a JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope `{ jsonrpc: "2.0", id?, method, params }` dispatched on `method`. Method names are the 1.0 PascalCase forms; the legacy 0.3 slash-strings are still accepted as tolerant aliases:
- `SendMessage` (alias `message/send`) — params: `{ message, configuration?, metadata? }`; returns an envelope whose `result` is `{ task }`.
- `SendStreamingMessage` (alias `message/stream`) — params: same; returns a `text/event-stream` of A2A 1.0 StreamResponse events.
- `GetTask` (alias `tasks/get`) — params: `{ id | taskId, historyLength? }`; returns a Task envelope.
- `ListTasks` — params: `{ pageSize?, pageToken?, status?, includeArtifacts? }`; returns `{ tasks, nextPageToken, pageSize, totalSize }` (cursor-paginated).
- `CancelTask` (alias `tasks/cancel`) — params: `{ id | taskId }`; returns a Task envelope.
- `SubscribeToTask` (alias `tasks/resubscribe`) — params: `{ id }`; returns a `text/event-stream` of A2A 1.0 StreamResponse events.
Each streaming `data:` line is a JSON-RPC response carrying a `StreamResponse` oneof (`statusUpdate` / `artifactUpdate`); errors stream as a JSON-RPC error envelope (`google.rpc.Status` form). The body is validated by the handler (not by request-schema middleware) so JSON-RPC error envelopes (`-32700`, METHOD_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_PARAMS) are preserved. Authentication is an A2A surface key (Authorization: Bearer a2a_xxx or X-API-Key header) or a Clerk JWT (dashboard testing).

200.description: JSON-RPC response envelope (unary methods) or an SSE event stream (SendStreamingMessage, SubscribeToTask).
